all.en: As a food blogger, I was invited to review the new restaurant opened by a famous celebrity chef.
all.zh: 身為美食部落客，我受邀去評論一間由知名名廚開設的新餐廳。

all.en: The restaurant was decorated in a modern style with industrial lights and exposed brick walls.
all.zh: 餐廳以現代風格裝潢，搭配工業風燈具與裸露的磚牆。

all.en: I had heard a lot of hype about the chef, so my expectations were extremely high.
all.zh: 我聽過許多關於這位主廚的吹捧，所以期待非常高。

all.en: The waiter was friendly but seemed nervous, perhaps because the place had just opened.
all.zh: 服務生很親切，但似乎有些緊張，也許是因為餐廳剛開業。

all.en: My first course was a tuna tartare, which looked beautiful but tasted strangely bland.
all.zh: 我的第一道菜是鮪魚塔塔，看起來很漂亮，但味道卻意外平淡。

all.en: The main dish, a duck breast with orange sauce, was unfortunately overcooked and tough.
all.zh: 主菜是橙汁鴨胸，可惜煮得過熟又老。

all.en: However, the dessert was truly impressive, with layers of light cream and crispy caramel.
all.zh: 不過，甜點確實令人驚艷，有層次分明的輕奶油與酥脆焦糖。

all.en: The portions were quite small considering the very high prices on the menu.
all.zh: 考慮到菜單上的高昂價格，份量算是相當小。

all.en: I felt that the chef relied too much on his reputation instead of cooking with passion.
all.zh: 我覺得這位主廚過於依賴自己的名聲，而非用熱情去烹調。

all.en: In my review, I gave the restaurant three stars out of five and suggested improvements.
all.zh: 在我的評論中，我給這間餐廳五顆星裡的三顆，並提出改進建議。

all.en: A famous name can attract customers once, but only good food will bring them back.
all.zh: 響亮的名字也許能吸引顧客一次，但只有美味的食物才能讓他們再次光顧。

all.en: After my review was published online, it received over fifty thousand views in just three days.
all.zh: 我的評論在網路上發表後，短短三天內就獲得超過五萬次的瀏覽。

all.en: Many readers thanked me for being honest instead of writing another flattering review.
all.zh: 許多讀者感謝我誠實評論，而不是寫又一篇逢迎拍馬的文章。

all.en: However, the celebrity chef's team contacted my editor to complain about the article.
all.zh: 然而，那位名廚的團隊聯繫了我的編輯，對這篇文章表達不滿。

all.en: They claimed my review was unfair and threatened to take legal action against our magazine.
all.zh: 他們聲稱我的評論不公平，並威脅要對我們的雜誌採取法律行動。

all.en: My editor stood firmly by my work and refused to remove or change a single word.
all.zh: 我的編輯堅定地支持我的作品，拒絕刪除或修改任何一個字。

all.en: Three months later, the chef quietly closed the restaurant due to declining customer numbers.
all.zh: 三個月後，這位主廚因為顧客數量下滑而悄悄結束了這家餐廳。

all.en: He gave an interview admitting that he had become too focused on fame and not enough on cooking.
all.zh: 他在受訪時承認，自己過於專注名聲，對烹調的投入卻不夠。

all.en: To my surprise, he invited me to dinner at his small home kitchen the following spring.
all.zh: 令我驚訝的是，隔年春天他邀請我到他家裡的小廚房共進晚餐。

all.en: There, he cooked a simple pasta dish that was honestly one of the best meals of my year.
all.zh: 在那裡，他煮了一道簡單的義大利麵，老實說是我那一年最棒的一餐。

all.en: That night reminded me that great food always comes from humility, not from celebrity status.
all.zh: 那一晚讓我體會到，偉大的美食總是源自於謙遜，而非名人光環。

all.en: That humble pasta dinner became the topic of my next blog post the following week.
all.zh: 那頓樸實的義大利麵晚餐，成了我隔週部落格文章的主題。

all.en: Readers responded warmly, and many said the chef's honest comeback touched them deeply.
all.zh: 讀者反應熱烈，許多人表示主廚誠實的回歸深深打動了他們。

all.en: Six months later, the chef quietly reopened a tiny twelve-seat restaurant in a small alley.
all.zh: 六個月後，這位主廚在一條小巷裡悄悄重新開設了一家僅十二個座位的小餐廳。

all.en: He cooked every single dish himself and personally greeted each guest at the door.
all.zh: 他親手烹調每一道菜，並親自在門口問候每位客人。

all.en: I returned to review the new place and gave it a glowing five-star recommendation.
all.zh: 我再次前往評論這家新店，並給予閃亮的五星推薦。

all.en: The food was simple, seasonal, and clearly cooked with passion in every careful detail.
all.zh: 料理樸實、依循時令，每個細節都顯露出滿滿的熱情。

all.en: Customers waited two months for a reservation, but the chef never raised his prices.
all.zh: 客人要等兩個月才能訂到位，但主廚從未調漲價格。

all.en: We became unlikely friends, occasionally meeting to share simple meals at his market stall.
all.zh: 我們成了意想不到的朋友，偶爾在他的市場攤位一起分享簡單的餐點。

all.en: He often told young chefs that humility was the most important ingredient in any kitchen.
all.zh: 他常告訴年輕廚師，謙遜是任何廚房中最重要的食材。

all.en: My honest review had unexpectedly helped a celebrity become a true chef once again.
all.zh: 我那篇誠實的評論，意外地幫助一位名人重新成為真正的廚師。
